verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He who hath seen Me, hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The Prophet said to the Jews, “Thou hadst the countenance of a harlot, thou wert shameless towards all men.” ( Jer. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now it seems fitting to use this expression not only against that city, but against all who shamelessly set their faces against the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when Philip said to Christ, “Show us the Father,” He replied, “Have I been so long time with you, and hast thou not known Me, Philip?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet there are some Who even after these words separate the Father from the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What proximity dost thou require closer than this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed from this very saying some have fallen into the malady of Sabellius.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But let us, leaving both these and those as involved in directly opposite error, consider the exact meaning of the words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Have I been so long time with you, and hast thou not known Me, Philip?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then? replieth Philip, “Art thou the Father after whom I enquire?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “No,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On this account He said not, “hast thou not known Him,” but, “hast thou not known Me,” declaring nothing else but this, that the Son is no other than what the Father is, yet continuing to be a Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But how came Philip to ask this question?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Christ had said, “If ye had known Me, ye should have known My Father also” ( c. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 7 ), and He had often said the same to the Jews.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since then Peter and the Jews had often asked Him, “Who is the Father?” since Thomas had asked Him, and no one had learnt anything clear, but His words were still not understood; Philip, in order that He might not seem to be importunate and to trouble Him by asking in his turn after the Jews, “Show us the Father,” added, “and it sufficeth us,” “we seek no more.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet Christ had said, “If ye had known Me, ye should have known My Father also,” and by Himself He declared the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Philip reversed the order, and said, “Show us the Father,” as though knowing Christ exactly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But Christ endureth him not, but putteth him in the right way, persuading him to gain the knowledge of the Father through Himself, while Philip desired to see Him with these bodily eyes, having perhaps heard concerning the Prophets, that they “saw God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But those cases, Philip, were acts of condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore Christ said, “No man hath seen God at any time” ( c. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 18 ); and again, “Every man that hath heard and hath learned from God cometh unto Me.” ( c. vi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have neither heard His voice at any time, nor seen His shape.” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And in the Old Testament, “No man shall see My face, and live.” ( Ex. xxxiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What saith Christ?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Very reprovingly He saith, “Have I been so long time with you, and hast thou not known Me, Philip?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He said not, “hast thou not seen,” but, “hast thou not known Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Why,” Philip might say, “do I wish to learn concerning Thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At present I seek to see Thy Father, and Thou sayest unto me, hast thou not known Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What connection then hath this with the question?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Surely a very close one; for if He is that which the Father is, yet continuing a Son, with reason He showeth in Himself Him who begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then to distinguish the Persons He saith, “He that hath seen Me hath seen the Father,” lest any one should assert that the same is Father, the same Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For had He been the Father, He would not have said, “He that hath seen Me hath seen Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then did He not reply, “thou askest things impossible, and not allowed to man; to Me alone is this possible”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because Philip had said, “it sufficeth us,” as though knowing Christ, He showeth that he had not even seen Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For assuredly he would have known the Father, had he been able to know the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He saith, “He that hath seen Me, hath seen the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any one hath seen Me, he shall also behold Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is of this kind: “It is not possible to see either Me or Him.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For Philip sought the knowledge which is by sight, and since he thought that he had so seen Christ, he desired in like manner to see the Father; but Jesus showeth him that he had not even seen Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if any one here call knowledge, sight, I do not contradict him, for, “he that hath known Me,” saith Christ, “hath known the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet He did not say this, but desiring to establish the Consubstantiality, declared, “he that knoweth My Essence, knoweth that of the Father also.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “And what is this?” saith some one; “for he who is acquainted with creation knoweth also God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet all are acquainted with creation, and have seen it, but all do not know God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, let us consider what Philip seeks to see.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it the wisdom of the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it His goodness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not so, but the very whatever God is, the very Essence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To this therefore Christ answereth, “He that hath seen Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now he that hath seen the creation, hath not also seen the Essence of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “If any one hath seen Me, he hath seen the Father,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now had He been of a different Essence, He would not have spoken thus.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But to make use of a grosser argument, no man that knows not what gold is, can discern the substance of gold in silver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For one nature is not shown by another.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore He rightly rebuked him, saying, “Am I so long with you?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Hast thou enjoyed such teaching, hast thou seen miracles wrought with authority, and all belonging to the Godhead, which the Father alone worketh, sins forgiven, secrets published, death retreating, a creation wrought from earth, and hast thou not known Me?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Because He was clothed with flesh, therefore He said, “Hast thou not known Me?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Thou hast seen the Father; seek not to see more; for in Him thou hast seen Me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If thou hast seen Me, be not over-curious; for thou hast also in Me known Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Believest thou not that I am in the Father?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “I am seen in that Essence.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The words that I speak, I speak not of Myself,”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the exceeding nearness, and the proof of the one Essence?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “The Father that dwelleth in Me, He doeth the works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How, beginning with words, doth He come to works? for that which naturally followed was, that He should say, “the Father speaketh the words.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But He putteth two things here, both concerning doctrine and miracles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Or it may have been because the words also were works.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then doeth He them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In another place He saith, “If I do not the works of My Father, believe Me not.” ( c. x.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: How then saith He here that the Father doeth them?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To show this same thing, that there is no interval between the Father and the Son.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What He saith is this: “The Father would not act in one way, and I in another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed in another place both He and the Father work; “My Father worketh hitherto, and I work” ( c. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 17 ); showing in the first passage the unvaryingness of the works, in the second the identity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if the obvious meaning of the words denotes humility, marvel not; for after having first said, “Believest thou not?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He then spake thus, showing that He so modeled His words to bring him to the faith; for He walked in their hearts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Believe that I am in the Father and the Father in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Ye ought not, when ye hear of ‘Father’ and ‘Son,’ to seek anything else to the establishing of the relationship as to Essence, but if this is not sufficient to prove to you the Condignity and Consubstantiality, ye may learn it even from the works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Had the, “he that hath seen Me, hath seen My Father,” been used with respect to works, He would not afterwards have said,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Or else believe Me for the very works’ sake.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And then to show that He is not only able to do these things, but also other much greater than these, He putteth them with excess.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For He saith not, “I can do greater things than these,” but, what was much more wonderful, “I can give to others also to do greater things than these.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Verily, verily, I say unto you, He that believeth on Me, the works that I do shall he do also; and greater works than these shall he do, because I go to the Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That is, “it now remaineth for you to work miracles, for I go away.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Then when He had accomplished what His argument intended, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Whatsoever ye shall ask in My Name, that will I do, that the Father may be glorified in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou again that it is He who doeth it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I,” saith He, “will do it”; not, “I will ask of the Father,” but, “that the Father may be glorified in Me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In another place He said, “God shall glorify Him in Himself” ( c. xiii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 32 ), but here, “He shall glorify the Father”; for when the Son shall appear with great power, He who begat shall be glorified.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But what is, “in My Name”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: That which the Apostles said, “In the Name of Jesus Christ, arise and walk.” ( Acts iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For all the miracles which they did He wrought in them, and “the hand of the Lord was with them.” ( Acts xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “I will do it,” He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou His authority?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The things done by means of others Himself doeth; hath He no power for the things done by Himself, except as being wrought in by the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And who could say this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But why doth He put it second?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To confirm His own words, and to show that the former sayings were of condescension.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the, “I go to the Father,” is this: “I shall not perish, but remain in My own proper Dignity, and Am in Heaven.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: All this He said, comforting them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since it was likely that they, not yet understanding His discourses concerning the Resurrection, would imagine something dismal, He in other discourses promiseth that He will give them such things, soothing them in every way, and showing that He abideth continually; and not only abideth, but that He will even show forth greater power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then follow Him, and take up the Cross.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For though persecution be not present, yet the season for another kind of death is with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Mortify,” it saith, “your members which are upon earth.” ( Col. iii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then quench concupiscence, slay anger, abolish envy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This is a “living sacrifice.” ( Rom. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This sacrifice ends not in ashes, is not dispersed in smoke, wants neither wood, nor fire, nor knife.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it hath both fire and a knife, even the Holy Spirit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Using this knife, circumcise the superfluous and alien portion of thy heart; open the closedness of thine ears, for vices and evil desires are wont to stop the way against the entrance of the word.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The desire of money, when it is set before one, permits not to hear the word concerning almsgiving; and malice when it is present raises a wall against the teaching concerning love; and some other malady falling on in its turn, makes the soul yet more dull to all things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then do away these wicked desires; it is enough to have willed, and all are quenched.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For let us not, I entreat, look to this, that the love of wealth is a tyrannical thing, but that the tyranny is that of our own slackmindedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Many indeed say that they do not even know what money is.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this desire is not a natural one; such as are natural were implanted in us from the first, from the beginning, but as for gold and silver, for a long time not even what it is was known.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Whence then grew this desire?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From vainglory and extreme slackmindedness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For of desires some are necessary, some natural, some neither the one nor the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For example, those which if not gratified destroy the creature are both natural and necessary, as the desire of meat and drink and sleep; carnal desire is natural indeed but not necessary, for many have got the better of it, and have not died.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But the desire of wealth is neither natural nor necessary, but superfluous; and if we choose we need not admit its beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At any rate, Christ speaking of virginity saith, “He that is able to receive it, let him receive it.” ( Matt. xix.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But concerning riches not so, but how?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “Except a man forsake all that he hath, he is not worthy of Me.” ( Luke xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What was easy He recommended, but what goes beyond the many He leaveth to choice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why then do we deprive ourselves of all excuse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The man who is made captive by some more tyrannical passion shall not suffer a heavy punishment, but he who is subdued by a weak one is deprived of all defense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what shall we reply when He saith, “Ye saw Me hungry and fed Me not”? ( Matt. xxv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 42 ); what excuse shall we have?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We shall certainly plead poverty; yet we are not poorer than that widow, who by throwing in two mites overshot all the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For God requireth not the quantity of the offering, but the measure of the mind; and that He doth so, comes from His tender care.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then, admiring His lovingkindness, contribute what is in our power, that having both in this life and in that which is to come obtained in abundance the lovingkindness of God, we may be able to enjoy the good things promised to us, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory for ever and ever.
